finding.message: XML parsing depends on a [user-provided value](1) without guarding against external entity expansion.

answers: The EXACT XML-parse sink is `etree.fromstring(await request.body(), parser)` on line 137. The parser object is `parser` and the input bytes/text passed to it is `await request.body()`.
The only assignment to the XML input on the path that reaches the parse call is on line 137: `doc = etree.fromstring(await request.body(), parser)`. The LAST assignment is also on line 137.

The value derives from user input (request body). The data-flow chain is: request (line 134) -> request.body() (line 137) -> await request.body() (line 137). The caller context shows this is a FastAPI endpoint, confirming the request is externally controlled.
The parser configuration is on line 136: `parser = etree.XMLParser(load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, no_network=False)`.
The parser is NOT safe-by-default. It ENABLES external entities (`resolve_entities=True`), DTD loading (`load_dtd=True`), and network access (`no_network=False`). This is configured on line 136.

The parser ENABLES external entities (line 136: `resolve_entities=True`) AND the input is user-controlled (line 137: `await request.body()`). The caller context confirms this is a reachable HTTP endpoint.
verification.data_flow: request (line 134) → request.body() (line 137) → await request.body() (line 137) → etree.fromstring(input, parser) (line 137)
verification.reasoning: The XML parser is explicitly configured to resolve external entities and load DTDs (line 136), and the input is directly from the user-provided request body (line 137) via a reachable FastAPI endpoint. This creates a clear XXE vulnerability.
